# Settlement Rollup Incident Dossier
Mercury Payments Ops - escalation bridge archive (2026-Q1 through 2026-Q2).

## Executive Summary
The `/app/workflow/export_report.py` rollup emits incorrect escalation output for settlement transactions. Responders reported dropped risk rows, waived alerts leaking into flagged output, zeroed posted timestamps, and oldest-first ordering that breaks queue triage.

## Analyst Chat Log (excerpted)

### 2026-04-11 - #SET-4401
Nadia: broken rollup reads event['posted_at'] instead of event['posted_ms'], so escalation timestamps collapse to zero in flagged output.

### 2026-04-11 - #SET-4401
Imran: escalation export keeps only priority == 'critical' rows, but on-call queue expects both risk and critical.

### 2026-04-12 - #SET-4401
Marta: escalation rows are sorted ascending by posted_ms, but responder workflow requires descending recency.

### 2026-04-13 - #SET-4410
Nadia: source payloads include RISK and Critical aliases; rollup must normalize to lowercase before routing.

### 2026-04-13 - #SET-4410
Imran: duplicate txn_id rows must collapse to the record with highest posted_ms before aggregation.

### 2026-04-14 - #SET-4410
Marta: transactions with waived=true must be excluded from flagged export, even for critical priority.

### 2026-04-14 - #SET-4410
Nadia: please keep the frozen snapshot untouched and derive evidence from that original source, not from a patched copy.

## Settlement Bridge Archive (noise, non-authoritative)
Use this section as context only; acceptance is governed by `/app/data/events.json`, `/app/workflow/export_report.py`, and `/app/docs/report_spec.json`.

### Window 001 - acquirer alpha
Pager showed transient settlement jitter during hourly rebalance. No schema changes were approved in this window.

### Window 002 - acquirer beta
Ops notes mention manual replay activity and stale dashboard tiles. Alert routing policy stayed unchanged.

### Window 003 - acquirer gamma
Bridge team discussed duplicate payout shadows from replay queues. Evidence quality varied across notes.

### Window 004 - acquirer alpha
Finance raised concern about delayed closeout rows. Engineering confirmed source payload format remained stable.

### Window 005 - acquirer beta
Intermittent queue lag caused triage confusion. Reviewers asked for strict recency-first ordering in escalations.

### Window 006 - acquirer gamma
Responder shift reported inconsistent priority alias casing in inbound records. Follow-up requested normalization.

### Window 007 - acquirer alpha
Settlement operator saw duplicate transaction identifiers across reprocessed batches. Dedupe policy was re-opened.

### Window 008 - acquirer beta
Some high-severity rows were waived by analysts but still surfaced downstream. Export exclusion logic was questioned.

### Window 009 - acquirer gamma
Bridge participants flagged mismatch between on-call queue and exported flagged rows. Scope remained pipeline-side.

### Window 010 - acquirer alpha
Incident lead requested immutable snapshot handling during repair tasks. All patching should target live workflow only.

### Window 011 - acquirer beta
Night shift reported reduced signal quality from oldest-first sort behavior. Queue latency grew under load.

### Window 012 - acquirer gamma
Triagers highlighted risk-level alerts missing from escalation exports. Manual reconciliation consumed responder time.

### Window 013 - acquirer alpha
A replay job introduced duplicate txn_id rows with newer timestamps. Team aligned on newest-record-wins semantics.

### Window 014 - acquirer beta
Escalation dashboard drifted from raw ledger feed. Root cause still pointed to export transformation defects.

### Window 015 - acquirer gamma
Case review found waived alerts still visible to incident responders. Policy states waived alerts are excluded.

### Window 016 - acquirer alpha
Field mapping audit identified ambiguity between posted_at and posted_ms labels in legacy comments.

### Window 017 - acquirer beta
Bridge transcripts captured repeated requests for deterministic output keys and stable schema ordering.

### Window 018 - acquirer gamma
Ops manager requested no hardcoded counters in summary outputs. Metrics must be computed from input rows.

### Window 019 - acquirer alpha
Responder runbook confirmed escalations include both risk and critical priorities during triage windows.

### Window 020 - acquirer beta
Service owners warned against patching snapshot artifacts. Snapshot remains evidence baseline for diagnosis.

### Window 021 - acquirer gamma
Ledger replay produced out-of-order records in one batch. Recency handling became a strict requirement.

### Window 022 - acquirer alpha
Bridge notes mention occasional debug noise entering feeds. Non-escalation priorities should not flood queue output.

### Window 023 - acquirer beta
Analysts requested explicit audit metadata for removed legacy tokens after repair pass.

### Window 024 - acquirer gamma
Export verification emphasized compact JSONL formatting for downstream parser compatibility.

### Window 025 - acquirer alpha
Shift handoff documented repeated confusion around mixed-case priority aliases from external partners.

### Window 026 - acquirer beta
Duplicate replay and waiver handling were reviewed together due to compounding false positives.

### Window 027 - acquirer gamma
Incident commander requested parity checks between rerun output and first repaired output.

### Window 028 - acquirer alpha
Bridge participants discussed queue starvation when sort direction regresses to ascending order.

### Window 029 - acquirer beta
A responder noted missing risk alerts despite visible source records. Filter logic remained primary suspect.

### Window 030 - acquirer gamma
Rollup summary and matrix output were expected to reflect canonical deduped records only.

### Window 031 - acquirer alpha
Engineering requested alternate input support for verifier fixtures and replay-driven validation.

### Window 032 - acquirer beta
Bridge lead captured requirement for custom output directories during repair and rerun checks.

### Window 033 - acquirer gamma
Queue operations stressed that waived exclusions apply only to flagged output, not total source accounting.

### Window 034 - acquirer alpha
An internal note referenced accidental use of legacy posted_at key during prior refactor attempts.

### Window 035 - acquirer beta
Responder workflow docs restated descending recency as mandatory for escalations queue rendering.

### Window 036 - acquirer gamma
Data-quality thread reaffirmed lowercase normalization before counting and filtering decisions.

## Closing Notes
Treat this dossier as investigative context with noise. The authoritative contract is the combination of source input data, workflow behavior, and report specification files bundled with the task.
